item.title: song
item.content:encoded: still haven't fully appreciated the skip in its entirety (i gotta reread it) but i love love love the song robert frost (first mal blum song i heard, i'm pretty sure), and the not-very-subtle nod to it was really fun to read. i am not a scp reviewer by any means so this is just my own extremely biased opinion but

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually quite like being confused most of the time!

+1, though I also don't get it at all.

this one will benefit from a declass at some point

item.title: Technical Team - Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Why is Gears's name spelled wrong in the header, but correctly in the tags?

I do not know why Gears's name was spelled the way it was in the header, but I added the doctor-gears tag as that character was mentioned in this article. It is spelled correctly because doctor-giers is not a valid tag.

As it is an incidental reference and not a core component of the article, if UraniumEmpire feels this tag is in error they may remove it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm finding myself very much in the same boat as my colleagues, DrSevere and RockTeethMothEyes. I feel like there's something that I'm missing here, and I also noticed the Gears spelling change. I will also add that the crosslink to #MassaTRUTHsetts doesn't clear much up for me to be perfectly honest, just more mud in the puddle. I'm gonna leave this as a novote for the time being because I just don't really get it.

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded:
I've been staring this down for two days now. I think that means this needs to happen.

+[Review] Click to Access
-[Click to Hide Text]

The first time I read this was around 6:00 AM on Sunday morning. The second time I read this was around 11:00 PM Sunday night. The third time I read this was just now. I say that I "read" these at these times, but what I mean is that I've been rereading them several times around those times. And I have come to two conclusions:

1. There is a lot to unpack here.
2. There is nothing to unpack here.

To echo DrSevere, it feels like I'm missing something each time I look through this. I'm trying to put all the pieces together to understand it. What's with all the stuff that Frost has packed in the Chrysler? What's with the corpses of these poets and sometimes Mal Blum? Why is Gears's name spelled wrong in the header, but correctly in the tags? I want to understand why any of this is happening and what the upvoters are getting that I'm not. Maybe it's because I've never read either of the crosslinks here that I'm not getting it.

Then, I tried to connect it all with the title: PlayerUnknown's Robert Frost. I think that's the biggest factor into this. By just accepting that, yes, all this stuff is happening, I find that I'm more accepting of it. I don't know if it counts as lowering my standards, but I feel like it doesn't because that's just a matter of interpretation. Some things just exist and do the same thing forever. It's pretty easy to forget that some works hinge on a certain flavor of absurdity to get by, and the Cack Hub parent page lends a hand to make that happen.

That's the conclusion I'm coming to here and what it bringing me to an upvote. It was a headache to get there, but sometimes, what you see is what you get. Robert Frost hits Robert Frost with a car for 33 years.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to RockTeethMothEyes or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I'm missing something, because I really don't get how the random elements of this tie together (do they tie together?). It seems like it's basically a less coherent version of the SCP-176 concept?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because it's about damn time we had a Mal Blum skip.

item.title: auth put
item.content:encoded:
Me, I guess I'm still something of an amateur
You'd think that I would know by now
Season 8 and I ain't figured it out

I'm very depressed and Mal Blum is very good.
